value.body:
## Context

New users need to understand profile setup, provider detection, repository selection, initialization, and first ticket creation without knowing Cycle internals.

## Outcome

First-run onboarding guides users through profile, agent providers, repository add/initialize, sync status, and creating or importing first tickets.

## Acceptance criteria

- Onboarding detects missing profile, no repositories, uninitialized repositories, and missing agent providers.
- Each step has clear actions, skip behavior, validation, and recovery after failure.
- Repository initialization explains local GitDB state and remote implications in concise UI copy.
- Completion state persists and settings allow revisiting setup diagnostics.
- Tests cover route/state transitions and failed repository/provider setup.

frontmatter.title: Polish onboarding and repository setup into a guided first-run flow

value.title: Polish onboarding and repository setup into a guided first-run flow
